relevant_files.role: Language registration hub; defines get_language_info() and get_ts_language() match arms for all languages; must add kotlin arm
Reference implementation for JVM-style language; shows ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY, extract_inheritance pattern
Extension mapping registry; EXTENSION_MAP must add .kt and .kts entries; supported_languages() must include kotlin
Feature flag definitions; must add lang-kotlin feature and tree-sitter-kotlin dependency
Workspace dependencies; must add tree-sitter-kotlin = 0.3.8 to [workspace.dependencies]
Development conventions: Conventional Commits, GPG signing, DCO sign-off, test requirements

conventions.commits: Conventional Commits (feat/fix/docs/refactor/test/chore scope); GPG signed (-S); DCO sign-off (--signoff); format: 'type(scope): subject'
conventions.testing: Unit tests in-file under #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-X"))]; AAA pattern (Arrange, Act, Assert); one happy path and one edge case per behavior; helper functions like parse_kotlin()
conventions.linting: cargo fmt --check; cargo clippy -- -D warnings; cargo deny check advisories licenses; cognitive complexity threshold 30
conventions.error_handling: thiserror for libraries; no .unwrap() in production code paths; proper error propagation via Result<T, E>

patterns: Feature-gated modules with #[cfg(feature = "lang-X")] on mod declaration and all references
SPDX header on every new .rs file: // SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2026 code-analyze-mcp contributors
Language module exports: ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY constants; optional DEFUSE_QUERY; optional extract_inheritance(node, source) -> Vec<String>
Query syntax: tree-sitter S-expression queries; capture names like @function, @class, @call, @import_path
Extension registry: EXTENSION_MAP with tuples (ext, language_name); case-insensitive lookup via eq_ignore_ascii_case

Language info struct: LanguageInfo { name, language, element_query, call_query, reference_query, import_query, impl_query, impl_trait_query, defuse_query, extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver, find_receiver_type, extract_inheritance }
Inheritance extraction: walk delegation_specifiers to collect superclass and interface names; distinguish constructor_invocation (superclass) from user_type (interface)

related_issues.title: feat(lang): add Kotlin grammar support
related_issues.relevance: Primary issue; defines acceptance criteria, node kinds, and implementation notes for Kotlin support

constraints: Kotlin grammar via tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 (fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin); no alternative implementations in scope
Feature flag lang-kotlin must be added to default feature set in crates/code-analyze-core/Cargo.toml
DEFUSE_QUERY not required by issue acceptance criteria (unlike Java, Go, C++); optional optimization
No impl_query or impl_trait_query needed (Kotlin uses inline delegation_specifiers, not separate impl blocks)
No extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver, find_receiver_type handlers needed (not in acceptance criteria)
Extensions .kt and .kts must be registered in EXTENSION_MAP behind lang-kotlin feature gate
All tests must pass with --features lang-kotlin; linting and formatting must pass
test_coverage: Existing test patterns: test_get_language_info_known/unknown in languages/mod.rs; test_language_for_extension_happy_path/edge_case in lang.rs. New kotlin.rs must include: parse_kotlin() helper, test_kotlin_element_query_happy_path (functions, classes), test_kotlin_element_query_classes (data class, interface, enum, object), test_kotlin_call_query, test_kotlin_import_query, test_kotlin_extract_inheritance (single superclass, multiple interfaces, mixed). All tests under #[cfg(all(test, feature = "lang-kotlin"))].

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter_kotlin::LANGUAGE constant; node kinds: function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration, call_expression, import_header, delegation_specifiers
library_findings.notes: fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin; High reputation (79.1 benchmark); 576 code snippets. Key node structures: delegation_specifiers contains constructor_invocation (superclass with parens) or user_type (interface, no parens); primary_constructor for class parameters; modifiers for data/companion keywords. Kotlin syntax mirrors Java but uses delegation_specifiers instead of extends/implements keywords.

library_findings.relevant_api: Query API (tree_sitter::Query, tree_sitter::QueryCursor); Language trait; Node navigation (child_by_field_name, named_child, start_byte, end_byte)
library_findings.notes: Workspace dependency; used by all language modules. Query strings are S-expression syntax; captures use @name syntax. Node field access via child_by_field_name for structured traversal.

approaches.name: Approach 1: Minimal Implementation (Issue Spec Only)
approaches.description: Implement exactly what issue #649 acceptance criteria require: ELEMENT_QUERY (function, method, class, data class, object, interface, enum), CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, extract_inheritance handler. No DEFUSE_QUERY. Feature flag lang-kotlin added to default set. Extensions .kt, .kts registered. Language arms added to get_language_info() and get_ts_language() in mod.rs. Comprehensive unit tests covering all node kinds and inheritance patterns.
pros: Exactly matches issue acceptance criteria; no scope creep
Minimal code footprint (~200 lines kotlin.rs + ~30 lines changes to mod.rs/lang.rs/Cargo.toml)
Faster to implement and review; lower risk of regressions
Consistent with Fortran approach (no DEFUSE_QUERY when not required)
Clear test coverage aligned to issue requirements
cons: No def/use analysis for Kotlin (unlike Java, Go, C++); limits some analysis capabilities
May require follow-up PR if DEFUSE_QUERY needed later

approaches.name: Approach 2: Full Feature Parity (Include DEFUSE_QUERY)
approaches.description: Implement issue spec plus DEFUSE_QUERY for complete def/use analysis. Model after java.rs DEFUSE_QUERY pattern: capture local variable declarations, assignments, updates, and identifier reads. Kotlin syntax differences: val/var keywords for declarations, assignment expressions, update operators. Extract all handlers even if None (for consistency with Java). Comprehensive test coverage including def/use scenarios.
pros: Full feature parity with Java/Go/C++ languages; future-proof
Enables def/use analysis for Kotlin codebases (variable tracking, data flow)
Demonstrates commitment to Kotlin as first-class language
Single PR covers all analysis capabilities; no follow-ups needed
cons: Scope expansion beyond issue acceptance criteria; requires justification
DEFUSE_QUERY adds ~30-50 lines; more complex query to validate
Longer review cycle; higher risk of query bugs
Issue explicitly marks DEFUSE_QUERY as not required; may be rejected as over-engineering

approaches.name: Approach 3: Phased Implementation (Minimal Now, DEFUSE Later)
approaches.description: Implement Approach 1 (minimal spec) with clear TODOs and comments indicating where DEFUSE_QUERY would go. Add lang-kotlin to default features. File issue or PR comment proposing DEFUSE_QUERY as follow-up. Structure kotlin.rs to make DEFUSE_QUERY addition trivial (e.g., comment showing query template). This allows merging quickly while signaling intent for future enhancement.
pros: Balances speed (merge Approach 1) with forward-thinking (signals DEFUSE_QUERY path)
Respects issue scope while documenting rationale for enhancement
Reviewers see clear upgrade path; easier to accept minimal PR
Separates concerns: core functionality vs. optimization
Demonstrates understanding of language design trade-offs
cons: Requires discipline to follow through on DEFUSE_QUERY PR
Two PRs instead of one; coordination overhead
May appear incomplete if follow-up PR never lands

recommendation: Approach 1 (Minimal Implementation). Rationale: (1) Issue #649 explicitly lists acceptance criteria without DEFUSE_QUERY; including it violates the principle of delivering what is asked, nothing more. (2) Kotlin is JVM-like but not identical to Java; DEFUSE_QUERY patterns may not port directly without validation. (3) Minimal approach unblocks Kotlin support immediately; DEFUSE_QUERY can be added in a follow-up PR if demand emerges. (4) Simpler review surface reduces merge friction. (5) Consistent with Fortran precedent (no DEFUSE_QUERY when not required). Implementation plan: (a) Create crates/code-analyze-core/src/languages/kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY (function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration), CALL_QUERY (call_expression), REFERENCE_QUERY (type_identifier), IMPORT_QUERY (import_header), extract_inheritance walking delegation_specifiers. (b) Add lang-kotlin feature to crates/code-analyze-core/Cargo.toml default set. (c) Add tree-sitter-kotlin = 0.3.8 to root Cargo.toml [workspace.dependencies]. (d) Register .kt, .kts in lang.rs EXTENSION_MAP. (e) Add kotlin arm to get_language_info() and get_ts_language() in languages/mod.rs. (f) Add mod kotlin; declaration. (g) Write comprehensive unit tests. (h) Verify cargo test --features lang-kotlin and cargo clippy pass.
